title: Onsemi to sell two manufacturing facilities as part of cost-cutting strategy
gist: Onsemi is divesting two fabs under its 'Fab Right' strategy—selling its Tarlac, Philippines site to Taiwan's Greatek Electronics (close in 3-6 months) and another site to Sweden's Silex Microsystems (close January 2028)—targeting ~$35 million in annual cost savings fully realized by 2028.

insights.claim: Onsemi, a supplier of power and sensing semiconductors for EVs, industrial automation and AI data centres, agreed to sell two chipmaking facilities to reduce costs, improve efficiency and strengthen margins under its 'Fab Right' strategy.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: Signals Onsemi prioritizing margin and footprint optimization over volume; a template for asset-light rationalization among analog/power players.
insights.quote: as part of an effort to reduce costs, improve efficiency and strengthen profit margins under its ongoing “Fab Right” manufacturing strategy

insights.claim: Onsemi expects the two divestments to generate approximately $35 million in annual cost savings, with initial savings beginning in 2027 and full benefit realized by 2028.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: Quantifies the margin uplift and timeline—$35M annual savings by 2028 is a concrete figure to model against.
insights.quote: Onsemi expects the two divestments to generate annual cost savings of approximately $35 million.

insights.claim: Onsemi will sell its Tarlac, Philippines facility to Taiwan-based packaging-and-test specialist Greatek Electronics, expected to close within three to six months with a long-term supply agreement to ensure production continuity.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: Greatek gains an OSAT footprint plus a captive Onsemi supply contract; illustrates OSAT consolidation of divested back-end assets.
insights.locator: Onsemi has reached an agreement to sell its facility in Tarlac

insights.claim: Onsemi will divest a second manufacturing site to Sweden-based Silex Microsystems, with the transaction expected to finalize in January 2028 and an extended transition to migrate products to other Onsemi facilities.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: The long 2028 close and product migration suggests specialized MEMS/sensing capacity moving to Silex; watch for continuity risk during transition.
insights.locator: The chipmaker has also signed an agreement

insights.claim: Onsemi did not disclose the financial terms of either transaction.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: Undisclosed terms limit valuation read-through; the savings figure is the only hard financial anchor available.
insights.quote: The company did not disclose the financial terms of either transaction.
